title: Session display Phase 3 ‚Äî Detach (detachable windows)
body:
## Requirement

**Session display Phase 3 ‚Äî Detach: detachable session windows.** Move a session into its own OS window (draggable to a 2nd monitor), session keeps running; reattach back into the main window.

- **Legacy stays the default** (non-breaking); detach is an **action**, not a third exclusive mode. Controlled via setting `global.sessionDisplayMode`.
- Builds on **Phase 1 tabs (#6)** + **Phase 2 settings window (#7)** (both done) ‚Äî uses the multi-window pattern proven there.
- Central change: route `terminal-data` per session to the owning window (sidebar/attention events stay in the main window).

Detailed design and implementation plan ‚Üí comment.

title: Windows: use bundled conpty.dll (useConptyDll) to fix stale/duplicated terminal rows
body:
On Windows, the terminal occasionally renders stale/duplicated rows ‚Äî most visibly the Claude CLI working-status (spinner) line stacking twice (e.g. two `* Word‚Ä¶ (time ¬∑ tokens)` rows at once). A manual resize heals it. Related: #100 (stacked status line), #85 (prompt/status line renders wrong).

**Root cause (evidence):** the stale row is present in xterm's *buffer* (a pure re-render does not heal, proven via the F5 refresh probe in #100), so the bad bytes arrive from upstream. Our `pty.spawn` calls use the OS in-box ConPTY (conhost), which is known to mis-handle rapid cursor-up + erase-line redraw cycles ‚Äî exactly what the CLI spinner does. The same symptom class is reported across all ConPTY hosts, independent of shell (PowerShell/cmd alike):

- anthropics/claude-code#13113 ‚Äî "Status line spinner creates multiple rows instead of updating in place"
- anthropics/claude-code#14630, anthropics/claude-code#16578, anthropics/claude-code#14599
- zed-industries/zed#52184 ‚Äî "TUI applications (Claude Code) render duplicate/ghost frames on Windows"
- microsoft/vscode#232353, microsoft/vscode#227137 ‚Äî duplicated lines; mitigated by `terminal.integrated.windowsUseConptyDll`

A resize heals because ConPTY then emits a full repaint from its own buffer, overwriting the stale row. The base shell choice (PowerShell) is not a factor ‚Äî ConPTY sits between every shell and xterm.

**Requirement:** spawn PTYs with node-pty's `useConptyDll: true` on Windows so the bundled conpty.dll (Windows Terminal codebase, v1.23.251008001, years of rendering fixes ahead of in-box conhost) is used instead of the OS ConPTY ‚Äî the same escape hatch VSCode exposes as `windowsUseConptyDll`. Because the node-pty flag is marked experimental, expose it as an advanced setting ("Windows ConPTY: Bundled (recommended) / System") so users can fall back to the system ConPTY without a rebuild. Packaging already ships the DLL (node-pty is asarUnpacked).

Keep this issue open until the fix is confirmed in daily use. Rollback: set the setting to System, or revert the commit referenced in the implementation comment.

title: orphaned hidden projects
body:
## Requirement

**Bug: orphaned hidden projects** ‚Äî `hiddenProjects` (switchboard DB setting) is **not** cleaned up when a project is removed (project management / from Claude); `get-hidden-projects` (`main.js:611`) returns the raw list without reconciling against `.claude.json`/existence ‚Üí the add-project hidden list shows removed projects (verified: several already-removed projects kept showing up, none still in `.claude.json`). Fix: prune `hiddenProjects` on project remove **and** filter `get-hidden-projects` against `.claude.json`. Files: `main.js`.

title: Terminal: prompt/status line renders wrong (doubled or blank), heals on resize
body:
Master issue for the **wrongly-rendered prompt/status line** bug (consolidates #64).
An in-place-rewritten TUI row in an active Claude session renders incorrectly and only
a terminal **resize heals it**. Two faces of the same class:

- **Doubled line** ‚Äî an upper stale copy stays while the lower copy updates.
  - Working-status / spinner line (e.g. `‚ú≥ Boogieing‚Ä¶`), mid-stream (was #64).
  - Input / prompt line (`> ‚ñå`).
- **Blank line(s)** ‚Äî the prompt frame (`>`) and the status line (`ctx / model / path`,
  `auto mode on`, `%`, `A 15`) are **not painted at all**; only a prior line + a lone
  cursor remain. Heals on resize.

**Renderer-agnostic ‚Äî reproduces with WebGL OFF (DOM renderer)**, so this is NOT a WebGL
/ texture-atlas problem (see the 2026-07-07 re-assessment comment). Not a fit/geometry
overshoot either (#59 class). The data is in the buffer; it is held or mis-painted before
/ while reaching the screen. A resize heals it because it forces a full re-layout + repaint.

**Expected:** any in-place-rewritten line paints faithfully; a single, live-updating line;
no blanks; no manual resize needed.

Keep this issue **open until the fix is confirmed in daily use** ‚Äî not deterministically
reproducible, so green tests do not verify it. Root-cause analysis (prime suspect: the
app-level DEC-2026 sync-update guard), suspects, rollback, and known risks are in the comments.

title: Terminal: working-status line renders stacked/duplicated (rare) ‚Äî distinct from #85 garble
body:
## Requirement

The Claude working-status line occasionally renders **stacked/duplicated** ‚Äî several
successive status frames sit on top of each other (e.g. three `Hashing‚Ä¶` lines with
*different* content: `22 24`, `16s ‚Üì401`, `11s ‚Üì138 ¬∑ thought for 5s`) instead of the
single line overwriting in place. A resize heals it. **Occurs rarely.** Seen on build
`main @ 7e473e7` (which already carries the DPR-refit fix), WebGL off (DOM renderer).

This is **distinct from #85** (that was the horizontal char-drift *garble* = xterm.js#6015,
addressed by the DPR-refit). This is **vertical duplication** ‚Äî old status rows not cleared
when the TUI rewrites the status line ‚Äî a different mechanism.

**Candidate:** xterm.js#5801 (OPEN) ‚Äî "ED2 (`\x1b[2J`) inside a DEC 2026 synchronized update
block resets viewportY, causing scroll yank during streaming redraws." Matches stacked status
frames during sync redraws. To be confirmed.

**Expected:** a single, in-place-updating status line; no stacking.

## Diagnosis (in progress)

Temporary probe added on a debug branch (never merged): **F5** = pure `terminal.refresh(0, rows-1)`
(render repaint), **F8** = `safeFit` (geometry re-fit) on the focused session. Press each on the
stacked ghost:
- F5 clears it ‚Üí dirty-row render-ghost ‚Üí a refresh-heal fixes it.
- F5 fails, F8/resize clears it ‚Üí geometry/re-fit class.
- Neither ‚Üí buffer/sync-viewport (xterm.js#5801) ‚Üí upstream / deeper.

Keep open until the mechanism is identified.

title: Port doctly: fix duplicate projects (encoding)
body: Port/evaluate doctly upstream `fix/duplicate-projects-encoding` ‚Äî duplicate projects caused by path encoding. Likely relevant to us. Source: doctly/switchboard @ fix/duplicate-projects-encoding.

title: Worktree/session history navigation
body:
## Requirement

**Worktree/session history navigation** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî Hotkey (e.g. Alt+‚Üê/‚Üí) to step forward/back through the most recently selected sessions/projects (MRU stack). Fits the porting workflow with many worktrees/branches. Pure renderer nav over the existing session switch.

title: Per-session/project color + custom title
body:
## Requirement

**Per-session/project color + custom title** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî freely selectable accent color per session/project row + optional short title for quick visual separation with many parallel sessions. Distinction: `customizing-colors.md` is global theming, here **per entry**. May use existing favorites/groups infrastructure.

title: "Dismiss All" for notifications
body:
## Requirement

**"Dismiss All" for notifications** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî a button/action that acknowledges all open in-app notifications / attention markers at once. Complements the Attention inbox (#13). Small, pure renderer + possibly state reset.

title: Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)
body:
## Requirement

**Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî open a repo/project on a remote host via SSH and manage it in Switchboard like a local one: session scan, terminal (PTY over SSH), file/diff access. **Large** ‚Äî needs its own detailed plan (transport, auth via Bitwarden SSH agent, remote `~/.claude/projects` scan, PTY bridging, watcher replacement). Clarify scope/architecture before implementation.

title: Agent badges: which tool runs where (evaluate)
body:
## Requirement

**Agent badges: which tool runs where (evaluate)** _(source: supacode)_ ‚Äî per session/row a badge showing the **agent type** (Claude / Codex / Copilot CLI / OpenCode) + live presence, hook-driven. Partial redundancy with #13 (status chips, but Claude only). Only makes sense together with #42 (Multi-LLM). **First evaluate** whether standalone or folded into #13.

title: Multi-LLM / multi-agent support (Codex, ‚Ä¶)
body:
## Requirement

**Multi-LLM / multi-agent support (Codex, ‚Ä¶)** ‚Äî alongside Claude Code, launch & manage other coding CLIs as a session type: **Codex**, GitHub Copilot CLI, OpenCode, and others. Affects session start (command/args per agent), detection/scan (not just `~/.claude/projects`), possibly a dedicated session-cache source. **Large**, needs its own detailed plan; foundation for #41.

title: Detect subagent spawn via .meta.json (not only .jsonl)
body:
## Requirement

**Detect subagent spawn via `.meta.json` (not only `.jsonl`)** ‚Äî when spawning a task subagent, Claude Code immediately writes `<parent>/subagents/agent-<id>.meta.json` (`agentType`, `description`, `toolUseId`, `spawnDepth`), but the `.jsonl` transcript only once the agent produces output. A spawned agent that did not (yet) run to producing output (queued, parent turn ends/is interrupted) has **only** `meta.json` ‚Üí `detectSubagentTransitions`/`enumerateSessionFiles` (which only scan `subagents/*.jsonl`) never see it (observed 2026-07-02: session `876c2b28`, "Feldtexte W6" agents ‚Äî meta identical to runnable W5 agents, but without jsonl; parent last at 22:26, spawns at 22:28). **No special background-agents format, no data loss ‚Äî pure visibility.** Fix: also scan `subagents/*.meta.json` ‚Üí placeholder subagent row (name from `description`, status "spawned/empty/aborted") as soon as the meta is there; upgrade to a full row when the `.jsonl` arrives. Files: `read-session-file.js` (`enumerateSessionFiles`), `session-transitions.js`, `session-cache.js`, subagent rendering in `public/sidebar.js`.

title: Grid layout a11y + DOM tests
body:
## Requirement

**Grid layout a11y + DOM tests** ‚Äî bonus addition to #04 (code done, beyond the Spec-08 acceptance criteria). (a) **Keyboard operation** for card resize/reorder: currently only pointer drag (`startCardResize`/`startCardDrag`), no keyboard path; `navigateGrid` only moves focus, the resize handle is `aria-hidden`. To add: resize the focused card via `Shift`+arrow, a "move mode" for reordering, ARIA live announcements. File: `public/grid-view.js`. (b) **DOM/integration tests** for resize/drag/persistence/group-drop ‚Äî so far only the pure model is tested (`test/grid-layout.test.js`), the interaction path is untested; needs a jsdom or Electron E2E harness (the project runs `node --test` without a DOM).

title: Per-session API key override
body:
## Requirement

**Per-session API key override** _(split off from #47)_ ‚Äî optional password field in the session dialogs that injects `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` **only transiently** per session into the PTY `env` (`open-terminal`, `main.js`) ‚Äî **never** in the command string or the settings DB. Persistence only via `saved_variables` `secret` (`safeStorage`). Picker layer from `brianstanley` @ `fb81d18`. **Net-new** (we are OAuth/Claude-only) ‚Äî before implementing, clarify whether it's even wanted.

title: Review 2026-07: code consolidation ‚Äî duplicated logic and dead code
body:
# Code consolidation: duplicated logic and dead code

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason. Pure refactoring: no behavior change, each consolidation must keep tests green.

## Duplications

- `claude-config.js:74-189` ‚Äî `setProjectTrust` / `removeProjectEntry` / `renameProjectEntry`: 3√ó identical read‚Üíparse‚Üí`.bak`‚Üítmp‚Üírename block ‚Üí shared `mutateClaudeConfig(fn)`.
- `main.js:1977` / `:2566` / `:2253` ‚Äî settings cascade (default‚Üíglobal‚Üíproject) written out 3√ó (resolveShellTypeForProject, open-terminal IIFE, get-effective-settings) ‚Üí `effectiveSetting(key, projectPath)` helper.
- `main.js:2427` vs. `:2444` ‚Äî read-session-jsonl / read-subagent-jsonl: identical "file‚Üíentries[]" parse block ‚Üí shared `readJsonlEntries(path)`.
- `public/dialogs.js:235-327` vs. `:386-461` ‚Äî permission-mode grid (modes array, `renderModeGrid`, grid click handler, overlay/key wiring) duplicated 1:1 between New and Resume dialog ‚Üí shared `buildPermissionModeDialog()`.
- `public/dialogs.js:62-69`, `:196-203`, `public/app.js:1773-1780` ‚Äî "inject pending session into cachedProjects/cachedAllProjects" block 3√ó ‚Üí `injectPendingSession(session, projectPath)`.
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:724-733`/`819-829`/`333-341` (toolResultMap) and `:757-767`/`:848-858`/`:495-503` (fullscreen overlay) ‚Äî transcript render logic duplicated 3√ó between `showJsonlViewer`, `showSubagentTranscript`, agent expand ‚Üí shared `renderTranscript(entries, container)`.
- `public/sidebar.js:2386-2479` vs. `:2484-2553` ‚Äî drag scaffold (threshold, ghost, moveGhost, cleanup, onUp) copied twice (session drag vs. project drag) ‚Üí generic `startPointerDrag(opts)`.
- `public/app.js:2101-2175` ‚Äî tab switch hides viewers per branch with 7+ hand-maintained `style.display='none'` lists (already diverged between branches) ‚Üí `hideAllViewers()` + show target explicitly.
- `public/app.js:2512-2519` vs. `public/settings-panel.js:1191-1195` ‚Äî usage-threshold clamping implemented twice with identical semantics ‚Üí one helper in utils.
- `workers/search-query.js:64,78-80` vs. `db.js:370,1040-1042` ‚Äî `FTS_QUERY_MAX_CHARS` + escape/`title:` logic duplicated ("must match" comment instead of a shared module) ‚Üí shared `search-query-util.js`.
- `shell-profiles.js:40-44` vs. `:128-133` ‚Äî Git-Bash candidate list maintained twice ‚Üí shared constant.
- `workers/scan-projects.js:10-23` vs. `session-cache.js:48-70` ‚Äî `readFolderFromFilesystem` (deriveProjectPath ‚Üí enumerate ‚Üí readSessionFile) exists twice; drift risk ‚Üí shared Electron-free helper.
- `public/codemirror-setup.js:56-68` vs. `:109-117` ‚Äî identical match-scan loop in `searchHighlighter` and `findAll()` ‚Üí shared `findMatches(doc, term)`.
- `public/terminal-manager.js:689-704` vs. `:727-734` ‚Äî `linkHandler.activate` and the WebLinksAddon callback carry the same logic (button guard + `file://` branch) ‚Üí one shared `activateLink(event, uri)`.

## Dead code / cleanup

- `read-session-file.js:336` ‚Äî `readSessionDisplayHeader` is unused (see also the sync-IO issue, where it should become used again ‚Äî coordinate).
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:14-52`, `:285-301` ‚Äî dead live-tail infrastructure: `liveSubagents` only ever filled, `data-subagent-watch-key` never set, `activeWatchId` never assigned. Remove or finish the feature.
- `sqlite-busy-retry.js:22` ‚Äî unreachable `throw lastErr` (last attempt rethrows in the catch) ‚Üí remove.
- `derive-project-path.js:47` ‚Äî signature takes 1 argument but ‚â•6 call sites pass a second `folder` (main.js:873,1455,1638, session-cache.js:51,449,569, workers/scan-projects.js:12) ‚Äî dead argument suggests unused semantics ‚Üí clean call sites.
- `public/plans-memory-view.js:70-76` ‚Äî active marking via text comparison of all `.session-id` (breaks with same-named files) while `openMemory:273` correctly uses `data-filepath` + `CSS.escape` ‚Üí same data-attribute technique.
- `public/style.css` ‚Äî ~15 top-level selectors defined more than once (e.g. `.variable-secret-pill` 7664+7681, `.session-stop-btn` 2007+2071, `.grid-card` 2√ó); complementary today but collision-prone ‚Üí consolidate per selector.

## Acceptance

- Each item consolidated or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green; zero behavior change (pure refactor).

title: Make the terminal LRU cap configurable
body:
The renderer keeps at most 12 live xterm instances (`TERMINAL_LRU_CAP` in `public/terminal-manager.js`); the least-recently-shown closed session beyond the cap is destroyed (soft cap: running and active sessions are never evicted).

Since WebGL is the default renderer, the cap also serves as the GL-context budget (Chromium allows ~16 per process; a lost context falls back to the DOM renderer).

Expose the cap as a setting for users with many parallel sessions and enough RAM. Constraints: keep 12 as the default; values above ~14 should hint that terminals beyond the GL budget silently fall back to the DOM renderer.

Deferred from #81 (was rejected there as "defer until the cap is hit").

title: Session tags: add a discoverable entry point (can't add the first tag)
body:
## Problem

Session tags exist (display chips via `decorateSessionItem`, editor via `editTags` in `bookmarks-tags.js`) but are effectively unusable: the **only** way to open the tag editor is clicking an **existing** tag chip. A session with no tags shows no chip, so there is **no way to add the first tag** from the UI ‚Äî no button, no context-menu entry.

## Requirement

Add a discoverable entry point to edit a session's tags, e.g. an "Edit tags‚Ä¶" item in the session right-click/context menu (and/or a small add-tag affordance). Opening it should call the existing `editTags(session)` dialog.

title: Port haydng: timeline time format 24h for CI locales
body:
## Requirement

Port haydng fix `8f06f1e` ‚Äî "Fix timeline time format for CI locales (24-hour clock)". Small, self-contained; makes the per-session timeline render times in 24h regardless of locale (avoids AM/PM ambiguity + CI-locale flakiness).

Source: `haydng/main` @ `8f06f1e`. Cherry-pick / adapt to our timeline viewer (`public/session-timeline.js`).

title: Port haydng: keep groups visible in running-only mode
body:
## Requirement

Port the bugfix part of haydng `17dba2a` (v0.2.7) ‚Äî **keep user groups visible when the "running only" filter is active**. Currently a group with no running session can vanish under running-only.

Source: `haydng/main` @ `17dba2a`. **Cherry-pick only the groups-visibility fix ‚Äî NOT the "React renderer foundation" that ships in the same commit** (our renderer is deliberately vanilla JS; see CLAUDE.md). Isolate the relevant sidebar/grid filter change.

title: Evaluate: agent teams (multi-agent orchestration)
body: Evaluate porting an agent-teams feature (multi-agent orchestration). Two forks have it: kreaddis `agent-teams-proto` and ivandobsky `feat/agent-teams`. Compare both, decide scope. Relates to #41/#42.

title: Evaluate: multi-sidebar panes
body: Evaluate porting aaaron `feat/multi-sidebar-panes` ‚Äî multiple sidebar panes. Source: aaaronmiller/switchboard @ feat/multi-sidebar-panes.

title: Evaluate: local Whisper voice input
body: Evaluate porting ivandobsky `feat/local-whisper-voice` ‚Äî local Whisper voice control/input. Source: ivandobskygithub/switchboard @ feat/local-whisper-voice.

title: Evaluate: rich analytics
body: Evaluate porting ivandobsky `feat/rich-analytics` ‚Äî richer analytics. Source: ivandobskygithub/switchboard @ feat/rich-analytics. Compare with our existing Activity Stats.

title: Evaluate: brianstanley cursor-ui-refresh
body: Evaluate brianstanley `cursor-ui-refresh` ‚Äî UI refresh. Source: brianstanley/switchboard @ cursor-ui-refresh. We already ported brianstanley's Variables + preview panel; check what's new/portable.

title: Port doctly: fix ai-title session name
body: Evaluate doctly upstream `fix/ai-title-session-name`. Source: doctly/switchboard @ fix/ai-title-session-name.

title: Port doctly: fix linux multi-arch build
body: Evaluate doctly upstream `fix/linux-multi-arch-build` (win target is x64-only here; check relevance). Source: doctly/switchboard @ fix/linux-multi-arch-build.

title: Optional second tray icon showing Claude usage (live percentage)
body:
Add an optional **second system-tray icon** that shows current Claude usage, enabled via Settings (off by default).

## Requirement
- A live-rendered tray icon showing the current usage percentage, styled like a usage monitor: a **colored progress ring** or a **badge** with the number baked into the icon, color-coded by the existing warn/critical thresholds (green ‚Üí amber ‚Üí red).
- **Tooltip** with the details we already show in the status bar (5h / 7d, extra usage, reset time).
- Small **context menu**: show/hide the window, open Stats, refresh, quit.
- **Cross-platform**: macOS, Windows, Linux.
- **Reuse the usage data the app already has** ‚Äî the same values the status bar renders. **No separate polling loop / no extra network fetch** for the tray; it updates whenever the existing status-bar usage updates.
- Enabled + styled via a **Settings** toggle (icon style ring/badge). When off, the second tray is not created; toggling off destroys it.

Reference behaviour: a small pystray + Pillow usage-monitor tray that draws a ring/badge with the percentage and threshold colors.

title: Terminal: report bundled-ConPTY capabilities to xterm (windowsPty.buildNumber)
body:
Follow-up to #114. xterm.js is told the PTY backend via `windowsPty: { backend: 'conpty', buildNumber: <OS build> }` (terminal-manager.js) so it can track ConPTY's reflow/wrapping behavior. Since #114 the PTY runs on node-pty's **bundled** conpty.dll (Windows Terminal codebase), whose capabilities are far newer than the OS build number implies ‚Äî on older Windows 10 hosts xterm would assume legacy ConPTY quirks that the bundled DLL no longer has, mismatching its wrapping/reflow heuristics.

**Requirement:** when the effective ConPTY backend is `bundled` (setting `conptyBackend`, default), report a build number to xterm that reflects the bundled DLL's capabilities (i.e. a high/modern build number) instead of the host OS build. When the setting is `system`, keep reporting the real OS build. Needs a small plumbing change: the renderer must know which backend is effective (e.g. expose via preload alongside `windowsBuildNumber`).

Low urgency: on modern Windows 11 hosts the OS build number is high enough that the hint is already effectively correct.

title: Terminal: evaluate conptyInheritCursor for PTY spawns
body:
Follow-up to #114. node-pty supports `conptyInheritCursor: true` (maps to `PSEUDOCONSOLE_INHERIT_CURSOR` in `CreatePseudoConsole`), which makes the new pseudo-console inherit the cursor position instead of starting at 0,0. VSCode passes it for its terminals to avoid the initial cursor jump / first-line overdraw when a terminal attaches.

**Requirement:** evaluate whether our spawn path benefits (both spawn sites in main.js ‚Äî Claude session + plain terminal). Watch for the known trade-off: with inherit-cursor, ConPTY emits a cursor-position request (CPR, `ESC[6n`) on startup and blocks briefly until the terminal answers ‚Äî xterm.js answers CPR, but the response arrives through our write-coalescing pipeline, so verify no startup stall or stray `;R` characters appear (a known failure mode in other hosts). Adopt only if it demonstrably improves attach behavior.

title: Terminal: evaluate VSCode-style shell integration (OSC 633) for plain terminals
body:
Idea from the VSCode draw-path comparison (#87/#114 context). VSCode's shell integration injects a shell profile hook that emits OSC 633 sequences (command start / command end / exit code / cwd), enabling command tracking, "run recent command", per-command scrollback navigation, and precise prompt detection.

**Requirement:** evaluate whether a slim variant is worth it for our **plain terminals** (not Claude sessions ‚Äî the CLI is a fullscreen TUI where command tracking is meaningless): e.g. exit-code badges, jump-to-previous-command in scrollback, or more reliable "shell is idle" detection. Likely low value for this app's core use case; document a decision either way. Large feature ‚Äî only pursue with a concrete user-facing benefit.
